text.title: Coverage Types and Configuration
text.summary: Line, branch, path, and condition coverage explained; why branch is the default; tool configuration for Python and JS/TS.

rule.statement: Enable branch coverage as the minimum; line coverage alone misses untested else-branches and is a weaker signal.
rule.rationale: A test that hits an if-branch but never the else-branch shows 100% line coverage while leaving half the logic untested.

list.item: Line coverage: which lines were executed. Weakest — a line is covered even if only one branch of an if is taken.
Branch coverage: both true and false branches of every conditional must be exercised. Required minimum.
Path coverage: all combinations of branches. Exponential — impractical for most code; use for safety-critical logic only.
Condition coverage: each boolean sub-expression. Use for complex compound conditions (a and b or c).

section.title: Python Configuration

rule.statement: Set branch = true in pyproject.toml coverage config and fail_under = 80 globally; raise per-directory thresholds for critical paths.
rule.rationale: Branch=true is opt-in in coverage.py; without it the gate is weaker than it appears.

section.reference: pyproject.toml coverage section with branch, omit, fail_under, exclude_lines.

section.title: JavaScript/TypeScript Configuration

rule.statement: Set coverageProvider: 'v8' in Jest/Vitest for native branch coverage; set thresholds at global and per-directory levels.
rule.rationale: V8 provider is more accurate than Babel-instrumented coverage for modern ES modules and async code.

section.reference: Jest coverage config with global and per-directory thresholds.

rule.statement: Exclude: auto-generated code, abstract base classes, TYPE_CHECKING blocks, CLI entry points, and simple property getters from coverage.
rule.rationale: Testing generated or structural code provides false assurance; each pragma: no cover must be justified, never added to silence a threshold.

list.item: pragma: no cover on abstract method stubs and @abstractmethod
